proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=97, 93.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +41.4pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8690 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=97, wr=93.7%, edge +41.4pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=97, Wilson lower 0.869, +41.4pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Bull (shifting, day 2), so the cohort is dormant; portfolio has no triggered concentration (0.6479 below 0.80) and no Bear exposure, so promotion does not compound risk. Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-06-29) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: BULL_PROXY × Bear: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=BULL_PROXY, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +30.8pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.98 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.98 sits just below Tier S threshold; D-8 review of Day-6 forced-exit dominance could revise downward
Bear regime currently absent (Bull day 2, shifting from Choppy); cohort remains dormant until Bear re-emerges
approval would propagate redundant to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +30.8pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +30.8pp edge over BULL_PROXY baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.98 (<1.0). Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Bull (shifting, day 2), so the cohort is dormant; portfolio Bear exposure is 0 and regime concentration (0.6479) is below the 0.80 trigger, so promotion does not compound concentration risk and may diversify when Bear re-emerges. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-06-19 through 06-29) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_signal_type = UP_TRI (98.6%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_signal_type = UP_TRI; recommended action: Pause new UP_TRI entries until signal-type share drops below 0.85 via natural Day-6 exits; prioritize any non-UP_TRI / short-side or hedge candidates (e.g., index put or short-bias signal) to break the monoculture. Avoid adding to Bank or Energy specifically until those cohorts validate beyond Candidate tier.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: If the Bull regime flips to Bear or a sharp risk-off shock hits, all 70 UP_TRI longs unwind together with no offsetting short exposure and no validated cohort edge to justify holding through drawdown — correlated stop-out cascade across Bank (11), Infra (9), Energy (8), and Other (8) sectors simultaneously.

evidence: BULL_PROXY accounts for the only non-UP_TRI position (1/71) — book is 100% directional long
Top cell UP_TRI×Bull×Bank at 15.49% (11/71) matches cohort Bank×UP_TRI at Candidate tier (n=65) — unvalidated edge
Second cell UP_TRI×Bull×Energy at 11.27% (8/71) matches cohort Energy×UP_TRI at Candidate tier (n=30) — unvalidated edge
Regime mix Bull 64.79% / Choppy 35.21% — no single regime above 0.80, but a Bull→Bear flip would unwind the entire long-heavy book simultaneously
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new UP_TRI entries until signal-type share drops below 0.85 via natural Day-6 exits; prioritize any non-UP_TRI / short-side or hedge candidates (e.g., index put or short-bias signal) to break the monoculture. Avoid adding to Bank or Energy specifically until those cohorts validate beyond Candidate tier.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: If the Bull regime flips to Bear or a sharp risk-off shock hits, all 70 UP_TRI longs unwind together with no offsetting short exposure and no validated cohort edge to justify holding through drawdown — correlated stop-out cascade across Bank (11), Infra (9), Energy (8), and Other (8) sectors simultaneously.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: UP_TRI dominates the signal mix at 70/71 = 98.6% (well above the 0.85 threshold), making the book a near-monoculture directional long. Both concentrated cells (UP_TRI×Bull×Bank at 15.49% and UP_TRI×Bull×Energy at 11.27%) match only Candidate-tier cohorts — unvalidated edge. While regime concentration (Bull 64.79%) and individual cells stay below their respective thresholds, the signal-type monoculture on unvalidated cohorts is the actionable risk.
